content.text:
```mdl
@ Non‑Disclosure Agreement (NDA) – PJM – 11 August 2015
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_13_nda_1

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# The NDA talks about “Confidential Information” that is supplied by a
# Disclosing Party to a Receiving Party.  The information may be in written
# form (including electronic) or oral.  In either case the NDA requires
# the Disclosing Party to **expressly identify** the information as
# confidential – either by marking it “Confidential” (written) or by
# announcing its confidential nature and then providing a written
# summary (oral).  The hypothesis we want to check is exactly this
# requirement.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Simple enumerations
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
type InfoForm = Written | Oral

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Core entity: a piece of Confidential Information
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    form: InfoForm,                     -- written or oral
    is_marked: bool,                    -- “Confidential” label (written)
    is_announced: bool,                 -- oral announcement flag
    written_summary_provided: bool      -- written follow‑up for oral info
}

entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Parties (only needed for traceability – no rules depend on them directly)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
type Party = { name: string }

entity disclosing_party: Party
entity receiving_party: Party

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions (pure, for readability)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
func is_expressly_identified(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    case info.form:
        | InfoForm.Written():
            info.is_marked
        | InfoForm.Oral():
            info.is_announced and info.written_summary_provided
        | _: false

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules (deontic obligations)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Section 9 – Identification of Confidential Information
rule O disclose_expressly_identify_confidential_info:
    is_expressly_identified(confidential_info) always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Additional sanity rules (optional, help the solver)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rule O written_info_must_be_marked:
    (confidential_info.form = InfoForm.Written()) implies confidential_info.is_marked always

rule O oral_info_must_be_announced_and_written:
    (confidential_info.form = InfoForm.Oral()) implies
        (confidential_info.is_announced and confidential_info.written_summary_provided) always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hypothesis (the statement we want to verify)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# All Confidential Information shall be expressly identified by the Disclosing Party.
# In MDL this is captured by the rule `disclose_expressly_identify_confidential_info`.
# If the solver returns `unsat` for a model where a piece of Confidential
# Information is not marked/announced, the hypothesis is falsified.
```
